hypothesis: Block copolymers consisting of amine-rich polyamine blocks separated by hydrophobic spacers of at least six methylene units will exhibit at least 20% greater CO2 uptake at 298 K, 0.4 mbar CO2 and 40% relative humidity than linear polyamines with equal total amine density, due to suppressed competitive H2O adsorption and increased accessible free volume in amine-rich domains.
